title: scandix pecten-veneris subsp. macrorhyncha
intro: Scandix pecten-veneris subsp. macrorhyncha is a subspecies of the Scandix pecten-veneris plant, also known as Shepherd's Needle, a type of biennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Scandix pecten-veneris subsp. macrorhyncha include relief from digestive issues, kidney stones, and urinary tract infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
